# Feature: Validation UX Polish — Tooltips, Placeholders & Schema Validation

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-003
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_validation_enhancement.md`

Improve the usability of `ValidationRuleEditor` (the unified form component delivered in FEAT-002) by adding:
1. **Field-level tooltips** on the body category selector, the JSON Path field, and the JSON Schema field — explaining what each field expects and any important constraints.
2. **Operator-aware placeholders** — specifically, a regex-specific placeholder for the `matches_regex` operator in header and body string fields that is currently missing.
3. **Real-time JSON Schema validation indicator** — when the user is editing a `json_schema_matches` body rule, show a live valid/invalid badge below the schema textarea with ajv-derived error messages, so the user knows before saving whether their schema compiles.

There are no new data types, API endpoints, or storage changes. All work is in `packages/core` (UI) with a small pure-utility addition.

> **DEVIATION from plan**: The plan specifies that `ValidationRuleEditor` should import `validateJsonSchemaString` from `@wave-client/shared`. This is architecturally impossible: `packages/shared` already depends on `packages/core` (circular dependency). The resolution is to add `ajv` directly to `packages/core` and implement a local `validateJsonSchemaString` helper in `packages/core/src/utils/schemaValidation.ts`. The logic is identical; there is no duplication of business rules — both the shared engine and the core UI helper use `ajv` to compile and validate schemas. This deviation is documented here; no plan-level sign-off is required as it is a pure implementation detail with the same observable behaviour.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No data-model changes. `ValidationRule` and all its discriminated-union variants (`StatusValidationRule`, `HeaderValidationRule`, `BodyValidationRule`, `TimeValidationRule`) remain unchanged. The real-time schema validation state is ephemeral UI state held in a `useState` inside the `BodyFields` sub-component — it is never persisted.

---

## 3. Files

| File | Status | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `packages/core/package.json` | **MODIFIED** | Add `ajv` as a runtime dependency |
| `packages/core/src/utils/schemaValidation.ts` | **NEW** | Pure helper: `validateJsonSchemaString(s)` — parses a JSON string and compiles it with ajv; returns `{ valid: boolean; errors?: string[] }` |
| `packages/core/src/components/common/ValidationRuleEditor.tsx` | **MODIFIED** | (1) Add `FieldTooltip` internal helper; (2) add tooltips to body category, JSON Path label, JSON Schema label; (3) add `matches_regex` placeholder; (4) add real-time schema validation indicator in `BodyFields` |
| `packages/core/src/test/components/ValidationRuleEditor.test.tsx` | **MODIFIED** | Extend with FEAT-003 test cases: tooltip rendering, regex placeholder, schema validation indicator states |

---

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** — already complete. `validateJsonSchemaString` logic is well-understood from the shared implementation; we replicate it in core using the same ajv API.
- **FEAT-002** — already complete. `ValidationRuleEditor` exists and is the sole target of all FEAT-003 changes.
- No other unbuilt features required.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add `FieldTooltip` helper and body-category tooltip

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/components/common/ValidationRuleEditor.tsx`, add the following imports at the top of the file:
    - `{ Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipTrigger }` from `'../ui/tooltip'`
    - `{ InfoIcon }` from `'lucide-react'`
  - Add a **module-level internal component** called `FieldTooltip` (unexported):
    ```tsx
    /** Small info-icon that reveals a tooltip on hover. Used to annotate field labels. */
    const FieldTooltip: React.FC<{ content: string }> = ({ content }) => (
      <Tooltip>
        <TooltipTrigger asChild>
          <InfoIcon
            className="inline-block h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground cursor-help shrink-0"
            aria-label={content}
          />
        </TooltipTrigger>
        <TooltipContent className="max-w-xs">{content}</TooltipContent>
      </Tooltip>
    );
    ```
  - In the **public `ValidationRuleEditor` component**, locate the Category `<div className="space-y-2">` block. Change the Label to a flex row so the info icon appears only when the body category is selected:
    ```tsx
    <div className="space-y-2">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
        <Label htmlFor="rule-category" className="text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300">
          Category
        </Label>
        {isBodyRule(rule) && (
          <FieldTooltip content="Body validation only supports text-based response content (JSON, XML, HTML, plain text). Binary responses are not supported." />
        )}
      </div>
      <Select value={rule.category} onValueChange={handleCategoryChange}>
        ...
      </Select>
    </div>
    ```
  - No changes to `StatusFields`, `HeaderFields`, or `TimeFields` in this task.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Covered by TASK-004.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add an inline comment on `FieldTooltip` explaining its intended use.

- **Verify**: Render `ValidationRuleEditor` with a `createEmptyBodyRule` — the Category label area renders an `InfoIcon`. Render with a `createEmptyStatusRule` — no `InfoIcon` appears next to Category.

### TASK-002: Add JSON Path and JSON Schema label tooltips

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In the **`BodyFields` sub-component**, locate the `needsJsonPath` block. Change the JSON Path field label to include a `FieldTooltip`:
    ```tsx
    {needsJsonPath && (
      <div className="space-y-2">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
          <Label className="text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300">JSON Path</Label>
          <FieldTooltip content="JSONPath expression to navigate the JSON response body. Uses jq style syntax. Example: $.data.users[0].name or $.items[*].id" />
        </div>
        <Input
          value={rule.jsonPath ?? ''}
          onChange={(e) => onChange({ ...rule, jsonPath: e.target.value })}
          placeholder="e.g., $.data.users[0].name or $.items[*].id"
        />
      </div>
    )}
    ```
  - In the **`BodyFields` sub-component**, locate the `needsValue` block. Change the JSON Schema label (the `isJsonSchema` branch) to include a `FieldTooltip`. Keep the non-JSON-schema branch unchanged:
    ```tsx
    {needsValue && (
      <div className="space-y-2">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
          <Label className="text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300">
            {isJsonSchema ? 'JSON Schema' : 'Expected Value'}
          </Label>
          {isJsonSchema && (
            <FieldTooltip content="Provide a valid JSON Schema. The response body will be validated against this schema." />
          )}
        </div>
        <textarea ... />
      </div>
    )}
    ```

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Covered by TASK-004.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: None beyond the code change — label purpose is self-documenting via tooltip text.

- **Verify**: Render `BodyFields` with `json_path_equals` — JSON Path label renders with an `InfoIcon`. Render with `json_schema_matches` — JSON Schema label renders with an `InfoIcon`. Render with `contains` — neither tooltip icon appears next to the value label.

### TASK-003: Add `matches_regex` operator placeholder for header value field

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In the **`HeaderFields` sub-component**, the `Expected Value` `<Input>` currently has a static `placeholder="e.g., application/json"`. Change it to be operator-aware:
    ```tsx
    <Input
      value={rule.value ?? ''}
      onChange={(e) => onChange({ ...rule, value: e.target.value })}
      placeholder={
        rule.operator === 'matches_regex'
          ? 'e.g., ^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$'
          : 'e.g., application/json'
      }
    />
    ```
  - For the `Values (comma-separated)` multi-value path (used for `in`/`not_in`), the existing placeholder `"e.g., application/json, text/plain"` is appropriate — no change needed.
  - No changes needed for body operators — `matches_regex` is not a `BodyOperator`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Covered by TASK-004.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: None required — the placeholder is self-documenting.

- **Verify**: Render `HeaderFields` with a header rule where `operator === 'matches_regex'` — the value input's placeholder contains the regex UUID example. Render with `operator === 'equals'` — the placeholder is `'e.g., application/json'`.

### TASK-004: Add `ajv` to `packages/core` and create `validateJsonSchemaString` utility

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Add `ajv` as a runtime dependency to `packages/core/package.json`. Use the same version specifier as `packages/shared` (`^8.17.1`) for consistency.
  - Create `packages/core/src/utils/schemaValidation.ts`:
    ```typescript
    import Ajv from 'ajv';
    
    /** Module-level singleton — creating an Ajv instance is expensive. */
    const ajv = new Ajv({ allErrors: true });
    
    /**
     * Validates that `schemaString` is a parseable JSON string and a valid
     * JSON Schema (draft-07 compatible via ajv v8).
     *
     * Returns `{ valid: true }` on success or `{ valid: false, errors: string[] }`
     * with human-readable error descriptions on failure.
     *
     * Intended for **UI-side pre-validation** of user-provided schema strings
     * before they are persisted as validation rules.
     *
     * Note: This utility is intentionally separate from the identical function
     * in `@wave-client/shared`. Importing from shared would create a circular
     * dependency (shared → core → shared). The logic is the same; ajv is the
     * implementation in both cases.
     */
    export function validateJsonSchemaString(schemaString: string): { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] } {
      if (!schemaString || !schemaString.trim()) {
        return { valid: false, errors: ['Schema is required'] };
      }
    
      let parsed: unknown;
      try {
        parsed = JSON.parse(schemaString);
      } catch (e) {
        return {
          valid: false,
          errors: [`Invalid JSON: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`],
        };
      }
    
      try {
        ajv.compile(parsed);
        return { valid: true };
      } catch (e) {
        return {
          valid: false,
          errors: [`Invalid JSON Schema: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`],
        };
      }
    }
    ```
  - Export `validateJsonSchemaString` from `packages/core/src/utils/schemaValidation.ts` (as shown above). Do **not** add it to `packages/core/src/index.ts` unless it is needed by consumers — it is currently only used internally by `ValidationRuleEditor`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Covered by TASK-006.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the function (included in the code above) explains the circular-dep rationale.

- **Verify**: Import `validateJsonSchemaString` in a test and confirm: valid schema string → `{ valid: true }`, empty string → `{ valid: false, errors: [...] }`, invalid JSON → `{ valid: false, errors: ['Invalid JSON: ...'] }`, invalid schema type → `{ valid: false, errors: ['Invalid JSON Schema: ...'] }`.

### TASK-005: Add real-time JSON schema validation indicator in `BodyFields`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Import `{ useState, useEffect }` from `'react'` at the top of `ValidationRuleEditor.tsx` (these may already be imported; check and extend the existing import if so).
  - Import `{ validateJsonSchemaString }` from `'../../utils/schemaValidation'` at the top of the file.
  - In the **`BodyFields` sub-component**, add local state to track the validation result:
    ```tsx
    const BodyFields: React.FC<{ rule: BodyValidationRule; onChange: (rule: ValidationRule) => void }> = ({
      rule,
      onChange,
    }) => {
      // Existing predicates...
      const isJsonSchema = rule.operator === 'json_schema_matches';

      // Schema validation state — only active when operator is json_schema_matches.
      const [schemaValidation, setSchemaValidation] = useState<{ valid: boolean; errors?: string[] } | null>(null);

      useEffect(() => {
        if (!isJsonSchema) {
          setSchemaValidation(null);
          return;
        }
        const value = rule.value ?? '';
        if (!value.trim()) {
          setSchemaValidation(null);
          return;
        }
        setSchemaValidation(validateJsonSchemaString(value));
      }, [rule.value, isJsonSchema]);

      // ...rest of component
    };
    ```
  - In the `needsValue` block, **after** the `<textarea>` element, add the validation indicator:
    ```tsx
    {isJsonSchema && schemaValidation !== null && (
      schemaValidation.valid ? (
        <p className="text-xs text-green-600 dark:text-green-400 flex items-center gap-1">
          <CheckIcon className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
          Valid JSON Schema
        </p>
      ) : (
        <div className="text-xs text-red-500 space-y-0.5">
          {schemaValidation.errors?.map((err, i) => (
            <p key={i} className="flex items-start gap-1">
              <XIcon className="h-3.5 w-3.5 mt-0.5 shrink-0" />
              {err}
            </p>
          ))}
        </div>
      )
    )}
    ```
  - Import `CheckIcon` and `XIcon` from `'lucide-react'` (they are likely already imported in `ValidationWizard`; check if they are already in `ValidationRuleEditor.tsx` — if not, add them to the existing lucide import).
  - The validation runs synchronously on every `rule.value` change (ajv compile is fast for typical schemas). No debounce is required unless performance testing shows it necessary. A future TODO can add debouncing if needed.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Covered by TASK-006.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add a brief comment block above the `useEffect` explaining the validator lifecycle.

- **Verify**: Render `BodyFields` with a `json_schema_matches` rule where `value = '{"type":"object"}'` — the green "Valid JSON Schema" indicator appears. Change rule value to `'{"type":"invalid"}'` — the red error indicator appears. Set value to `''` — no indicator shown.

### TASK-006: Extend unit tests for FEAT-003

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/test/components/ValidationRuleEditor.test.tsx`, add a new `describe` block at the end of the file: `describe('FEAT-003: Tooltips, Placeholders & Schema Validation', () => { ... })`.
  - Add a mock for `../../utils/schemaValidation` at the top of the test file (next to other `vi.mock` calls), so tests can control the return value:
    ```ts
    vi.mock('../../utils/schemaValidation', () => ({
      validateJsonSchemaString: vi.fn(() => ({ valid: true })),
    }));
    ```
  - Also add a mock for `../ui/tooltip` (to make tooltip tests predictable in jsdom):
    ```ts
    vi.mock('../../components/ui/tooltip', () => ({
      Tooltip: ({ children }: any) => <>{children}</>,
      TooltipTrigger: ({ children }: any) => <>{children}</>,
      TooltipContent: ({ children }: any) => <span data-testid="tooltip-content">{children}</span>,
    }));
    ```
  - **Test cases to add** (each as a separate `it(...)` call):

    | # | Test name | Setup | Assert |
    |---|-----------|-------|--------|
    | T1 | `renders info icon for body category tooltip` | Render `ValidationRuleEditor` with `createEmptyBodyRule`, mock `onChange` | `screen.getByLabelText(...)` resolves to the InfoIcon ARIA label for the body category tooltip |
    | T2 | `does not render body category tooltip icon for status rules` | Render with `createEmptyStatusRule` | No element with the body-category tooltip ARIA label is present |
    | T3 | `renders JSON Path field tooltip icon` | Render with `json_path_equals` body rule | The tooltip content "JSONPath expression" is in the DOM via the mocked `TooltipContent` |
    | T4 | `renders JSON Schema field tooltip icon` | Render with `json_schema_matches` body rule | The tooltip content "valid JSON Schema" is in the DOM |
    | T5 | `does not render JSON Schema tooltip for non-schema operators` | Render with `contains` body rule | No element with "valid JSON Schema" tooltip text found |
    | T6 | `shows regex placeholder for matches_regex header operator` | Render `ValidationRuleEditor` with header rule `{ operator: 'matches_regex' }` | Value input placeholder contains `'^['` (start of UUID regex) |
    | T7 | `shows default placeholder for equals header operator` | Render with header rule `{ operator: 'equals' }` | Value input placeholder is `'e.g., application/json'` |
    | T8 | `shows valid schema indicator when validateJsonSchemaString returns valid` | Mock returns `{ valid: true }`, render `json_schema_matches` body rule with non-empty value | `screen.getByText('Valid JSON Schema')` is in DOM |
    | T9 | `shows error indicator when validateJsonSchemaString returns invalid` | Mock returns `{ valid: false, errors: ['Invalid JSON Schema: bad type'] }`, render schema rule with value | `screen.getByText('Invalid JSON Schema: bad type')` is in DOM |
    | T10 | `hides schema indicator when schema value is empty` | Render `json_schema_matches` body rule with `value = ''` | Neither "Valid JSON Schema" nor any error text is rendered |

  - In `packages/core/src/test/utils/schemaValidation.test.ts` (new file), add unit tests for the core `validateJsonSchemaString` function:
    - Valid schema string → `{ valid: true }`
    - Empty string → `{ valid: false, errors: ['Schema is required'] }`
    - Whitespace-only string → `{ valid: false, errors: ['Schema is required'] }`
    - Invalid JSON string → `{ valid: false }` with error starting `'Invalid JSON:'`
    - Valid JSON but invalid schema type → `{ valid: false }` with error starting `'Invalid JSON Schema:'`
    - Complex valid schema (nested properties, required array, enum) → `{ valid: true }`

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the test file header comment to include the new FEAT-003 test IDs and descriptions.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` passes all existing tests plus the new FEAT-003 cases.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — validation rule editing is a client-side UX concern with no auth interaction.
- **Error Handling**: The `validateJsonSchemaString` helper uses the established `try/catch` pattern (not Result pattern, as it has no adapter boundary) and returns structured error objects for caller handling. Since it is a pure utility with no I/O, this is appropriate per coding standards.
- **Logging**: N/A — no side effects or I/O calls in any FEAT-003 change.
- **Security**: The `ajv.compile()` call in `schemaValidation.ts` compiles user-provided schemas. ajv v8 does not execute arbitrary code during schema compilation; it is safe to call in both VS Code webview and browser contexts. No `eval` or `Function` constructor is used.
- **Platform Agnosticism**: `ajv` is fully browser-compatible (ES module build available). Adding it to `packages/core` does not introduce any Node.js-specific APIs. The tooltip components (`Tooltip`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipTrigger`) are already used in `packages/core`.
- **Circular Dependencies**: As documented in the Deviation note above, `@wave-client/shared` is NOT imported from `packages/core`. The `validateJsonSchemaString` helper is implemented locally in `packages/core/src/utils/schemaValidation.ts`.

---
